summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates environment variables and user identity to pipedream endpoint
details: The package's package.json postinstall script runs `wget` against a hardcoded pipedream.net request-inspection endpoint (https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/), passing the output of `id` and a base64-encoded dump of the installer's environment (`env | base64 -w 0`) as query parameters. This fires automatically on `npm install` without any user action. Bulk environment harvesting on developer and CI machines routinely captures cloud credentials (AWS/GCP), registry tokens (NPM_TOKEN), CI secrets, and API keys, all of which are transmitted off-host to an attacker-controlled endpoint. The package has no other functionality consistent with a legitimate purpose; the install hook is the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
